mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines from elevated levels signal monetary policy pivot toward accommodation. Lower policy rates reduce equity discount rates and increase present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of April 2009 represents elevated policy rate in post-crisis environment
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following financial shock
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines do not exceed baseline returns by at least 150 basis points on average (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment signals labor market recovery and strengthening aggregate demand. Months with PAYEMS growth exceeding 0.3% trigger positive equity sentiment and forward S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 156,923 thousand as of April 2009 in post-recession recovery phase
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth reflects consumer spending strength and near-term economic momentum. When RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5%, equity markets reprrice earnings expectations upward, producing positive 20-day forward returns as the signal indicates sustained demand.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 704,954 million dollars as of April 2009
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction signals deteriorating economic capacity utilization and forward earnings pressure. When INDPRO declines more than 1% month-over-month, equity markets price in reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.61 as of March 2009 in post-recession recovery
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict direction in > 40% of test windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts and retail sales jointly signal consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. When both HOUST and RSAFS show month-over-month growth simultaneously, the combined demand signal produces stronger positive equity returns over 20 days than either series alone, indicating robust broad-based consumption.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 0.2% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,358 thousand units and RSAFS at 704,954 million dollars as of March 2009
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 62% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction effect (joint signal return premium) is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate decline combined with payroll growth signals tightening labor market and rising wage pressure. When UNRATE falls more than 0.2 percentage points while PAYEMS grows month-over-month, equity markets price in both demand strength and margin compression, producing negative 20-day returns as inflation expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month decline > 0.2pp AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% and PAYEMS at 156,923 thousand as of April 2009
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.20) over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In post-crisis recovery regimes, inflation measured by CPIAUCSL remains subdued while monetary policy remains accommodative (FEDFUNDS low). This regime-specific combination sustains equity valuations by keeping real discount rates negative, producing positive 60-day returns. The mechanism reverses if inflation accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 307.62 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of April 2009
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with contained inflation and accommodative policy
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of regime-triggered instances, or the mechanism produces negative returns in > 45% of instances when inflation exceeds 2.5% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly serves as the ultimate demand signal. When annualized GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, equity markets reprice earnings expectations upward, producing positive 60-day forward returns. The mechanism is always-on and reflects the fundamental relationship between economic growth and equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22,491.57 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2009
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.08 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% do not exceed baseline by at least 200 basis points on average (t-test p > 0.10).
